insight: In the FIFA World Cup 2026, Czechia's performance during hydration breaks has been a mixed bag, with the team managing to gain momentum in only half of the two breaks they've had, resulting in a slight decline in momentum overall. When Czechia was pressing before a hydration break, they tended to lose momentum at a higher rate, with an average decline of 0.300 per minute, and they were unable to maintain their momentum after the break. On the other hand, when they weren't pressing before a break, they were able to hold steady, with no change in momentum on average, suggesting that the breaks may have helped them regroup and prevent further declines. This suggests that Czechia may need to rethink their strategy when pressing before hydration breaks to avoid losing their edge.
